articles.title: Fight for Hormuz swings between missiles & diplomacy as Iran minister reaches Oman
articles.description: Iran's foreign minister Abbas Araqchi arrived in Oman to discuss ship passage safety. The United States seeks a public pledge for secure and free transit. Recent hostilities escalated after attacks on commercial tankers in the Gulf. Both sides have agreed to continue talks despite the ongoing conflict. Oil prices have risen globally due to the insecurity in the region.

articles.title: Tensions Rise as US and Iran Hold Gulf Talks Amid Intensified Conflict
articles.description: Iran's Foreign Minister visited Oman to discuss Strait of Hormuz passage as tensions flared with the US. Despite recent hostilities, talks are set to continue, although the ceasefire has been declared over. The conflict has increased global oil prices and escalated the risk of economic repercussions.

articles.title: High-Stakes Diplomacy: Iran-U.S. Tensions Over Hormuz
articles.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi met in Oman to discuss the safe passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz. Despite escalating tensions due to U.S.-Iran hostilities, both nations are continuing dialogue. Trump has warned Iran of retaliatory military action. The conflict has further driven up global oil prices.

articles.title: In reverse flow, India now supplies petrol to energy powerhouse Russia
articles.description: India's petrol is navigating its way to Russia via international traders rather than through direct sales channels. The damage to Russia's refining infrastructure has severely impacted its fuel production. This indirect trade underscores the ongoing shifts within the global energy market and the consequences of sanctions.
